scenarioStory.history: 6-year-old female with chronic bronchopulmonary dysplasia (former 25-week preemie), ventilator-dependent at baseline, with a chronic tracheostomy (4.0 mm cuffed, in place for 4 years). Parents report the child has been congested for 2 days and had thick secretions this morning. When mom attempted routine suctioning, the catheter would not pass. Child is now in acute distress with cyanosis.
scenarioStory.discussion: Apply the MATC Tracheostomy Management protocol to a plugged trach. Execute emergent trach change under time pressure. Use the DOPE mnemonic to troubleshoot trach patients. Leverage family expertise and the home go-bag. Recognize when oral intubation is a viable rescue
scenarioStory.course: Arrival — Severe Respiratory Distress via Tracheostomy: Ventilator-dependent pediatric tracheostomy patient with complete obstruction. Per protocol, in a peds trach patient in significant distress, the problem is PLUGGING or DISLODGEMENT until proven otherwise. Systematic DOPE troubleshooting: disconnect vent, pass suction, if catheter won't pass, emergently change the trach. → Emergent Tracheostomy Change: Crew commits to emergent tracheostomy replacement. Deflate cuff, remove ties, pull old trach, insert lubricated new trach with obturator, remove obturator, begin BVM ventilation, confirm placement. → Default — Successful Replacement: New trach in place. BVM confirms good ventilation with bilateral breath sounds and return of EtCO2 waveform. Child pinks up rapidly. Transport to pediatric ED.

patientInformation.patientCondition: Acute respiratory distress, unable to pass suction catheter through trach

scenarioEvents.name: Arrival — Severe Respiratory Distress via Tracheostomy
scenarioEvents.description: Ventilator-dependent pediatric tracheostomy patient with complete obstruction. Per protocol, in a peds trach patient in significant distress, the problem is PLUGGING or DISLODGEMENT until proven otherwise. Systematic DOPE troubleshooting: disconnect vent, pass suction, if catheter won't pass, emergently change the trach.

scenarioEvents.name: Emergent Tracheostomy Change
scenarioEvents.description: Crew commits to emergent tracheostomy replacement. Deflate cuff, remove ties, pull old trach, insert lubricated new trach with obturator, remove obturator, begin BVM ventilation, confirm placement.

scenarioEvents.name: Default — Successful Replacement
scenarioEvents.description: New trach in place. BVM confirms good ventilation with bilateral breath sounds and return of EtCO2 waveform. Child pinks up rapidly. Transport to pediatric ED.

checklist.title: Recognize pediatric trach patient in distress = plug or dislodgement until proven otherwise
Provide passive oxygenation over nose/mouth AND stoma
Attempt saline and suction catheter pass (size = 2x trach size)
If catheter will not pass, change the tracheostomy emergently with same-size replacement
Confirm new trach placement with waveform capnography
Bring the family go-bag and invite a parent to accompany
If replacement fails, try smaller size OR orally intubate from above if native airway present
Transport to pediatric ED with notification
Document existing trach size from flange imprint
